findings.title: CVE-2026-41242 — Protobuf.js Code Injection RCE Affects Millions of Node.js Applications (CVSS 9.4)
findings.summary: Critical code injection in protobuf.js allows RCE via malicious protobuf type definitions exploiting unsafe Function() constructor usage, with public PoC and patches available in versions 7.5.5 and 8.0.1.

noted.product: CMP Coming Soon & Maintenance WordPress Plugin
noted.summary: Arbitrary file upload and RCE via improper validation of downloaded ZIP files in theme update functionality (CVSS 8.8).
Code execution through terminal output parsing of SSH conductor protocol from untrusted sources (CVSS 6.9).
Stack buffer overflow in glob function via crafted directory structures and .editorconfig files (CVSS 8.6).
Compromised server exploits improper gzip filename validation to overwrite critical files on journalist workstations (CVSS 7.5).
Five authorization and information disclosure issues including improper variable redaction and DAG access controls.

noted.product: TBK DVRs / TP-Link EOL routers (Nexcorium Mirai variant)
noted.summary: THN and FortiGuard published full analysis of Nexcorium Mirai variant targeting legacy IoT devices with multi-architecture DDoS capabilities.

noted.product: AVideo, openDCIM, ChurchCRM, Selenium Grid (Metasploit modules)
noted.summary: Metasploit 04/17 wrap-up adds four new RCE exploit modules; Selenium Grid exposure in CI/CD environments is most notable.
International law enforcement seized 53 DDoS domains across 21 countries, arrested 4 individuals, and obtained 3M+ criminal user accounts.
NIST now prioritizes CVE enrichment for CISA KEV entries and critical infrastructure due to 263% surge in submissions.
